title: From primordial nucleosynthesis to rocky planet formation

shots.speech_text: The key measurement: Big Bang nucleosynthesis (BBN) at T+3 min produced H ~75%, He-4 ~25% by mass, D/H ~2.527×10⁻⁵ (Cooke et al., 2018, ApJ 855); Planck 2018 baryon density Ω_b h² = 0.02237 ± 0.00015. Results like this, produced by Planck Collaboration (ESA/NASA), transformed our understanding of From primordial nucleosynthesis to rocky planet formation.

shots.speech_text: From primordial nucleosynthesis to rocky planet formation is not just a phenomenon at the far edge of science. It connects directly to questions about our own origins. The s has implications that reach far beyond the laboratory.

shots.speech_text: We are only beginning to understand From primordial nucleosynthesis to rocky planet formation. And perhaps that's exactly the point — the universe keeps its most ancient secrets just within reach, if we keep looking.

guardrails: HELIX is voiceover only — no on-screen host, no synthetic presenter, no avatar.
All quantitative claims must include units and citation.
Uncertainty must be stated where present (e.g. 'approximately', 'within a margin of').
Do not present theoretical models as confirmed fact without qualification.
Do not fabricate measurements, paper titles, DOIs, or institutional affiliations.
Maintain wonder without hyperbole — the phenomena are extraordinary enough.
No speculation presented as established science.
HELIX does not cover history, biography, or language — those are DAVID's domain.
